title: Ledger Dot Mesh
prompt:
Create a production-ready Ledger Dot Mesh using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original background effect uses a quiet six-by-four dotted ledger field with one peach locator that moves when a reader chooses a coordinate. The grid should support foreground text rather than compete with it, and the selected coordinate must be stated in words below the controls. Use a light Bento composition with a #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE hairline border, 22px corners, a soft neutral shadow, #111111 type, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Peach is the only bright accent, and the card itself must use a solid fill, never a gradient.

Use native buttons in a labelled group, aria-pressed, and an aria-live polite confirmation. Click, Enter, and Space must move the locator among three named positions. Animate the locator with a spring using stiffness 280 and damping 24; fade the coordinate label in over 140ms ease-out. Keep all state local and deterministic; no timers, random values, storage, remote assets, network calls, canvas, SVG filters, or browser-global access. Use transforms and opacity for motion.

At 320px, retain an unclipped mesh and 44px controls; at detail size, allow the field to breathe. Preserve logical tab order, visible 3px focus rings, AA contrast, and written state in addition to the peach locator. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the locator and using only a brief opacity fade. Return complete client code, a pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [a,setA]=React.useState(1);const p=[['North','18%','20%'],['Center','48%','50%'],['South','78%','78%']];return
LOCATOR FIELD
{Array.from({length:24},(_,i)=>)}
{p.map((x,i)=>)}

Locator moved to {p[a][0]}.

}return ;})()
